articles.title: Socceroos' World Cup ends in heartbreak, but path forward now clear

children.content: The Socceroos exited the World Cup in the cruellest possible manner, on penalties to Egypt. But the roots of the defeat could be found in the 120 minutes of play, which clearly showed where Australia must improve.

image.altText: Jackson Irvine consoles Lucas Herrington

articles.title: Quick hits: Beach makes a stunning save and Socceroos teen feels the heat

children.content: An own goal cancelled out Egypt's early goal before a penalty shootout gamble by Tony Popovic ended in heartbreak for the Socceroos. Here are the quick hits from the Socceroos vs Egypt.

items.title: Government rejects all UN recommendations on LGBTQIA+ rights

children.content: The Albanese government has accepted the lowest number of recommendations made by the UN Human Rights Council in 20 years, with none protecting the LGBTQIA+ community.

items.title: As thin crowds at Trump's fair go viral, Americans reflect on July 4

items.title: When a fit young man disappears, he leaves behind a trail of strange clues

children.content: When the first major relationship of Jack McLennan's life implodes, he does what plenty of young men do: meets a mate for a quiet beer. But by morning, Jack's disappeared, leaving behind a series of mysterious clues and a devastated family.

items.title: Today Show ratings in 'freefall' as Channel Nine looks to replace Karl Stefanovic

children.content: The Today Show's ratings are in decline following the Nine Network's dumping of host Karl Stefanovic. Former head of News and Current Affairs at Seven, Nine, and 10, Peter Meakin says the audience will want to punish Nine, but things can turn around, even in breakfast TV.

children.content: Pub nights are becoming far less rowdy as young people swap big nights out for morning run clubs.
